is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study analyses P. falciparum isolates: 'These isolates were examined for point mutations in the genes of DHFR... and DHPS' in P. falciparum from Mauritania.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Table 1 reports DHFR codons 51, 59, 108 and DHPS codons 436, 437, 540, 581, 613 — all curated target positions.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 1 gives counts and percentages per codon per site, e.g. DHFR 51-isoleucine Aioun 10 (16.95), Kobeni 13 (12.62); DHPS 437-glycine Aioun 13 (22.03) of n=59.

sub_country_location.evidence: Frequencies reported separately for two towns: Aioun (n=59 pos) and Kobeni (n=101/103) in Hodh El Gharbi, south-east Mauritania.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Samples collected 'during an in vivo study on resistance to chloroquine in 1998' — a single collection year.

african_field_samples.evidence: Field/clinical samples from febrile patients at health centres in Aioun and Kobeni, Mauritania (African country).

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical field survey with PCR/RFLP analysis of collected blood samples.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Described as part of one of the first surveys on malaria drug resistance in Mauritania, collected during an in vivo chloroquine study in 1998; no named consortium or overlapping published dataset stated.

supplement.evidence: All per-codon frequencies present in Table 1 of the main text.

assessment.summary: Primary field study of P. falciparum antifolate-resistance markers in DHFR (51,59,108) and DHPS (436,437,540,581,613) from 162 PCR-positive samples collected in 1998 at two sites (Aioun n=59, Kobeni n=~101) in Hodh El Gharbi, south-east Mauritania. Table 1 provides extractable per-codon counts and percentages by site. Sub-national resolution and single collection year satisfy spatial and temporal criteria.
